import json
import re

files = {
    'sovereigns.promoted.js': '/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/sovereigns.promoted.js',
    'sovereigns.research.js': '/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/sovereigns.research.js'
}

for filename, filepath in files.items\(\):
    with open\(filepath, 'r'\) as f:
        content = f.read\(\)
    
    # Extract the people array
    people_match = re.search\(r'export const people = \\[\(.*?\)export const edges', content, re.DOTALL\)
    if people_match:
        people_str = '[' + people_match.group\(1\) + ']'
        
        # For JSON files with quotes, just clean up trailing commas
        people_str = re.sub\(r',\\s*\\]', ']', people_str\)
        people_str = re.sub\(r',\\s*}', '}', people_str\)
        
        try:
            people = json.loads\(people_str\)
            
            # Extract edges
            edges_match = re.search\(r'export const edges = \\[\(.*?\)\\];', content, re.DOTALL\)
            edges_str = '[]'
            edges_count = 0
            if edges_match:
                edges_str = '[' + edges_match.group\(1\) + ']'
                edges_str = re.sub\(r',\\s*\\]', ']', edges_str\)
                edges_str = re.sub\(r',\\s*}', '}', edges_str\)
                edges = json.loads\(edges_str\)
                edges_count = len\(edges\)
            
            # Count stats
            total_nodes = len\(people\)
            with_reign = sum\(1 for p in people if p.get\('re'\) and any\(p.get\('re', []\)\)\)
            without_reign = total_nodes - with_reign
            
            # Count by dynasty
            dynasties = {}
            for p in people:
                dy = p.get\('dy', 'Unknown'\)
                dynasties[dy] = dynasties.get\(dy, 0\) + 1
            
            print\(f"\\n=== {filename} Statistics ==="\)
            print\(f"Total nodes: {total_nodes}"\)
            print\(f"Nodes with reign dates: {with_reign}"\)
            print\(f"Nodes without reign dates: {without_reign}"\)
            print\(f"Total edges: {edges_count}"\)
            if dynasties:
                print\(f"Dynasties:"\)
                for dy in sorted\(dynasties.keys\(\)\):
                    print\(f"  {dy}: {dynasties[dy]}"\)
                
        except \(json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError\) as e:
            print\(f"\\nError parsing {filename}: {e}"\)

EOF)

Bash(__NEW_LINE_7afbf9728d2cd01d__ python3 << 'EOF'
import re
from collections import defaultdict, deque

# Read the data
with open\('/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/sovereigns.js', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)

# Parse edges - using regex to extract edge objects
edges_section = re.search\(r'export const edges = \\[\(.*?\)\\];', content, re.DOTALL\).group\(1\)
edge_objects = re.findall\(r'\\{t:"\(\\w+\)",s:"\(P\\d+\)",d:"\(P\\d+\)"[^}]*\\}', edges_section\)

print\(f"Total edges found: {len\(edge_objects\)}"\)

# Build graph
graph = defaultdict\(set\)
all_nodes = set\(\)

for edge_type, source, dest in edge_objects:
    graph[source].add\(dest\)
    graph[dest].add\(source\)
    all_nodes.add\(source\)
    all_nodes.add\(dest\)

# Add isolated nodes \(those with no edges\)
people_section = re.search\(r'export const people = \\[\(.*?\)\\];', content, re.DOTALL\).group\(1\)
person_ids = re.findall\(r'\\{id:"\(P\\d+\)"', people_section\)
isolated_nodes = set\(person_ids\) - all_nodes

print\(f"\\nBase dataset \(sovereigns.js\):"\)
print\(f"  Total nodes: {len\(person_ids\)}"\)
print\(f"  Nodes in edges graph: {len\(all_nodes\)}"\)
print\(f"  Isolated nodes \(no edges\): {len\(isolated_nodes\)}"\)
if isolated_nodes:
    print\(f"  Isolated node IDs: {sorted\(isolated_nodes\)[:10]}"\)

# Find connected components
visited = set\(\)
components = []

def bfs\(start\):
    queue = deque\([start]\)
    component = set\(\)
    visited.add\(start\)
    while queue:
        node = queue.popleft\(\)
        component.add\(node\)
        for neighbor in graph[node]:
            if neighbor not in visited:
                visited.add\(neighbor\)
                queue.append\(neighbor\)
    return component

for node in all_nodes:
    if node not in visited:
        comp = bfs\(node\)
        components.append\(comp\)

# Add isolated nodes as single-node components
for iso in isolated_nodes:
    components.append\({iso}\)
    visited.add\(iso\)

components.sort\(key=len, reverse=True\)

print\(f"\\nConnected components: {len\(components\)}"\)
print\(f"  Largest component size: {len\(components[0]\) if components else 0} nodes"\)
print\(f"  Top 10 component sizes:"\)
for i, comp in enumerate\(components[:10]\):
    print\(f"    Component {i+1}: {len\(comp\)} nodes"\)

# Analyze tree depth \(looking at parent-child relationships\)
parent_edges = []
for edge_type, source, dest in edge_objects:
    if edge_type == 'parent':
        parent_edges.append\(\(source, dest\)\)

print\(f"\\nParent-child relationships: {len\(parent_edges\)}"\)

# Build parent graph to calculate depths
children_map = defaultdict\(list\)
has_parent = set\(\)

for parent, child in parent_edges:
    children_map[parent].append\(child\)
    has_parent.add\(child\)

# Find roots \(nodes with no parents\)
roots = all_nodes - has_parent if all_nodes else set\(\)
print\(f"Root nodes \(no parents\): {len\(roots\)}"\)
print\(f"  Root node IDs: {sorted\(roots\)[:5]}"\)

# Calculate max depth from roots
def get_depth\(node, memo={}\):
    if node in memo:
        return memo[node]
    children = children_map.get\(node, []\)
    if not children:
        depth = 0
    else:
        depth = 1 + max\(get_depth\(child, memo\) for child in children\)
    memo[node] = depth
    return depth

max_depth = max\(\(get_depth\(root\) for root in roots\), default=0\)
print\(f"Maximum tree depth \(generations\): {max_depth}"\)

EOF)

Bash(__NEW_LINE_26be712f01c4fd7e__ python3 << 'EOF'
import re
from collections import defaultdict

# Read the data
with open\('/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/sovereigns.js', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)

# Parse all edges with their properties
edges_section = re.search\(r'export const edges = \\[\(.*?\)\\];', content, re.DOTALL\).group\(1\)

# Extract full edge objects
edge_objects = re.findall\(r'\\{[^}]*?"t":"?\(\\w+\)"?[^}]*?"s":"?\(P\\d+\)"?[^}]*?"d":"?\(P\\d+\)"?[^}]*?"c":"?\(\\w+\)"?[^}]*?\\}', edges_section\)

# Also try original format
edge_objects_orig = re.findall\(r'\\{t:"\(\\w+\)",s:"\(P\\d+\)",d:"\(P\\d+\)"[^}]*?c:"\(\\w+\)"', edges_section\)

if not edge_objects:
    edge_objects = edge_objects_orig

print\("=== Edge Type Analysis ==="\)

# Count by type
edge_types = defaultdict\(int\)
confidence_map = defaultdict\(lambda: defaultdict\(int\)\)

for edge in edge_objects:
    if len\(edge\) >= 4:
        etype = edge[0]
        source = edge[1]
        dest = edge[2]
        confidence = edge[3] if len\(edge\) > 3 else 'unknown'
        
        edge_types[etype] += 1
        confidence_map[etype][confidence] += 1
    
# Also count from simple regex
simple_edges = re.findall\(r'\\{t:"\(\\w+\)"', edges_section\)
print\(f"Total edges by simple scan: {len\(simple_edges\)}"\)

for etype in sorted\(set\(simple_edges\)\):
    count = simple_edges.count\(etype\)
    print\(f"  {etype}: {count}"\)

print\(f"\\n=== Confidence Grade Analysis ==="\)
# Scan for confidence grades
conf_grades = re.findall\(r'confidence_grade:?"\(\\\\w+\)"', edges_section\)
conf_simple = re.findall\(r'c:"\(\\w\)"', edges_section\)

print\(f"Confidence grades from 'c' field:"\)
conf_counts = defaultdict\(int\)
for c in conf_simple:
    conf_counts[c] += 1

for c in sorted\(conf_counts.keys\(\)\):
    print\(f"  {c}: {conf_counts[c]}"\)
    # Map to confidence levels
    mapping = {'c': 'confirmed', 'i': 'inferred', 'u': 'unconfirmed'}
    print\(f"    \(= {mapping.get\(c, 'unknown'\)}\)"\)

print\(f"\\n=== Edge Type Breakdown with Confidence ==="\)
# Get all edge_type,c pairs
edge_conf = re.findall\(r'\\{t:"\(\\w+\)"[^}]*?c:"\(\\w\)"', edges_section\)
ec_map = defaultdict\(lambda: defaultdict\(int\)\)
for etype, conf in edge_conf:
    ec_map[etype][conf] += 1

for etype in sorted\(ec_map.keys\(\)\):
    print\(f"{etype}:"\)
    for conf in sorted\(ec_map[etype].keys\(\)\):
        print\(f"  {conf}: {ec_map[etype][conf]}"\)

EOF)

import re

# Read the timeline data
with open\('/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/sovereigns.js', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)

# Parse birth and death years, and reign dates
people_section = re.search\(r'export const people = \\[\(.*?\)\\];', content, re.DOTALL\).group\(1\)

# Extract birth/death years
yb_values = re.findall\(r'yb:\(\\d+\)', people_section\)
yd_values = re.findall\(r'yd:\(\\d+\)', people_section\)

# Extract reign years
re_values = re.findall\(r're:\\[\\[\(\\d+\),\(\\d+\)\\]', people_section\)

yb_ints = [int\(y\) for y in yb_values]
yd_ints = [int\(y\) for y in yd_values]
re_starts = [int\(r[0]\) for r in re_values]
re_ends = [int\(r[1]\) for r in re_values]

all_years = yb_ints + yd_ints + re_starts + re_ends

print\("=== Timeline Extent Analysis ==="\)
print\(f"Birth years with data: {len\(yb_ints\)}"\)
if yb_ints:
    print\(f"  Range: {min\(yb_ints\)} - {max\(yb_ints\)}"\)
print\(f"\\nDeath years with data: {len\(yd_ints\)}"\)
if yd_ints:
    print\(f"  Range: {min\(yd_ints\)} - {max\(yd_ints\)}"\)
print\(f"\\nReign start years: {len\(re_starts\)}"\)
if re_starts:
    print\(f"  Range: {min\(re_starts\)} - {max\(re_starts\)}"\)
print\(f"\\nReign end years: {len\(re_ends\)}"\)
if re_ends:
    print\(f"  Range: {min\(re_ends\)} - {max\(re_ends\)}"\)

print\(f"\\nOverall timeline extent: {min\(all_years\)} - {max\(all_years\)}"\)

# Count nodes with different era relevance
with_yb = len\(yb_ints\)
with_yd = len\(yd_ints\)
with_re = len\(re_starts\)
print\(f"\\nNode data completeness:"\)
print\(f"  With birth year: {with_yb}/84"\)
print\(f"  With death year: {with_yd}/84"\)
print\(f"  With reign dates: {with_re}/84"\)

EOF)

Bash(__NEW_LINE_ffee475160d5bbac__ python3 << 'EOF'
import re

# Read base data
with open\('/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/sovereigns.js', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)

# Count sovereigns - those with 'n:' array containing reign numbers
people_section = re.search\(r'export const people = \\[\(.*?\)\\];', content, re.DOTALL\).group\(1\)

# Extract sovereign numbering
sovereigns = re.findall\(r'n:\\[\([^\\]]+\)\\]', people_section\)

print\("=== Sovereign Numbering Analysis ==="\)
print\(f"Total unique sovereign numbering entries: {len\(sovereigns\)}"\)

# Count how many have multiple numbers \(multiple accessions\)
multi_accessions = sum\(1 for s in sovereigns if ',' in s\)
print\(f"People with multiple reigns \(multiple 'n' numbers\): {multi_accessions}"\)

# Extract all sovereign numbers
all_numbers = []
for s in sovereigns:
    nums = [int\(n.strip\(\)\) for n in s.split\(','\)]
    all_numbers.extend\(nums\)

print\(f"Total sovereign instances \(counting multiple reigns\): {len\(all_numbers\)}"\)
print\(f"Highest sovereign number: {max\(all_numbers\) if all_numbers else 0}"\)

# Check if sovereigns are contiguous
all_numbers_sorted = sorted\(set\(all_numbers\)\)
print\(f"Unique sovereign numbers: {len\(all_numbers_sorted\)}"\)
if all_numbers_sorted:
    print\(f"  Range: {min\(all_numbers_sorted\)} - {max\(all_numbers_sorted\)}"\)
    # Check for gaps
    gaps = []
    for i in range\(min\(all_numbers_sorted\), max\(all_numbers_sorted\) + 1\):
        if i not in all_numbers_sorted:
            gaps.append\(i\)
    if gaps:
        print\(f"  Missing sovereign numbers: {gaps[:20]}"\)

# Compare with dynasty counts
print\(f"\\n=== Dynasty Sovereign Distribution ==="\)
dynasties = re.findall\(r'dy:"\([^"]+\)"', people_section\)
print\(f"Total people with dynasty: {len\(dynasties\)}"\)

# Count sovereigns per dynasty
dynasty_sovs = {}
for pattern in re.finditer\(r'dy:"\([^"]+\)"[^}]*?n:\\[\([^\\]]*\)\\]', people_section\):
    dy = pattern.group\(1\)
    ns = pattern.group\(2\)
    if ns.strip\(\):  # Has sovereign numbers
        if dy not in dynasty_sovs:
            dynasty_sovs[dy] = {'count': 0, 'accessions': 0}
        dynasty_sovs[dy]['count'] += 1
        dynasty_sovs[dy]['accessions'] += len\([n for n in ns.split\(','\) if n.strip\(\)]\)

for dy in sorted\(dynasty_sovs.keys\(\)\):
    print\(f"  {dy}: {dynasty_sovs[dy]['count']} sovereigns, {dynasty_sovs[dy]['accessions']} accessions"\)

EOF)

import re

# Read data
with open\('/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/sovereigns.js', 'r'\) as f:
    content = f.read\(\)

people_section = re.search\(r'export const people = \\[\(.*?\)\\];', content, re.DOTALL\).group\(1\)

# Find all people and check if they have reign numbers
people_with_n = re.findall\(r'id:"\(P\\d+\)"[^}]*?n:\\[\([^\\]]*\)\\]', people_section\)

sovereigns = sum\(1 for _, n in people_with_n if n.strip\(\)\)
non_sovereigns = sum\(1 for _, n in people_with_n if not n.strip\(\)\)

print\("=== Sovereign vs Non-Sovereign Analysis ==="\)
print\(f"Sovereigns \(with reign numbering\): {sovereigns}"\)
print\(f"Non-sovereigns \(family members, consorts\): {non_sovereigns}"\)
print\(f"Total: {sovereigns + non_sovereigns}"\)

# Identify some non-sovereigns
non_sov_ids = [pid for pid, n in people_with_n if not n.strip\(\)]
print\(f"\\nSample non-sovereign node IDs: {non_sov_ids[:10]}"\)

# Check office distribution
with open\('/Users/rush/Documents/codex_maldives_royal_geneaology/src/data/offices.js', 'r'\) as f:
    offices_content = f.read\(\)

office_ids = re.findall\(r"id: '\([^']+\)'", offices_content\)
office_kinds = re.findall\(r"kind: '\([^']+\)'", offices_content\)

print\(f"\\n=== Office Catalog ==="\)
print\(f"Total offices: {len\(office_ids\)}"\)

kind_counts = {}
for kind in office_kinds:
    kind_counts[kind] = kind_counts.get\(kind, 0\) + 1

print\(f"Office types:"\)
for kind in sorted\(kind_counts.keys\(\)\):
    print\(f"  {kind}: {kind_counts[kind]}"\)

EOF)
